theme.premise: a breezy seaside-boardwalk folktale of a young beachcomber who runs a tiny curiosity shop — combing the tide pools at dawn for shells, sea glass and driftwood, crafting them into trinkets, and ringing up a queue of cheerful seaside patrons by afternoon
theme.tone: sunny, bustling, lightly comic — honest-work satisfaction with a pinch of register-rush pressure
theme.setting: a small wooden boardwalk shop above bright tide pools, mid-morning summer light

concept.core_loop: A repeating day loop with one live decision per second in every phase. GATHER (tide timer): tap resource nodes in the tide pools — near pools give common shells fast, far pools give rare sea glass/pearls but the returning tide sweeps un-banked hauls, so the choice is 'push out for rares or bank what I have'. CRAFT: spend the limited haul on recipes — cheap fast-selling trinkets vs. expensive slow-sellers, steered by today's posted demand hints. SELL (cashier): patrons queue with item wants and draining patience meters; the player chooses WHO to serve next and grabs the matching item off the shelf — serve order vs. patience triage is the decision. Profits buy tool upgrades (bigger haul, rare access, longer tide window) and shop upgrades (more shelves, patron patience, busier queue). Difficulty ramps per day: more patrons, faster patience drain, and demand shifts toward higher-tier recipes needing rare materials — upgrades are the counter-pressure.
mechanics: day cycle with three phases: gather -> craft -> sell, then an upgrade/results screen
tide-pool gather phase: tappable resource nodes (shell/driftwood/sea glass/pearl) over a tide countdown; far nodes rarer; tide-return sweeps unbanked haul (bank action)
crafting screen: 5-6 recipes combining resources into sellable items with tiered prices; daily demand hints
shelf stocking: crafted items placed onto limited shop shelves
cashier sell phase: patron queue with wanted-item bubbles and patience timers; tap patron then tap matching shelf item to ring up; missed patience = patron leaves
gold economy: sales income, running day total
tool upgrade track: haul size, tide window, rare-node access
shop upgrade track: shelf slots, patience slow-down, patron traffic
per-day difficulty ramp: patron count, patience drain, demand tier
persistent save of gold + purchased upgrades + day number
game over is soft: a day with zero stock ends early; restart day available
concept.art_direction: sunny flat-cartoon seaside: sand-cream, sea-teal, coral-orange, driftwood-brown palette with rounded chunky shapes and bold outlines; background treatment: layered beach — warm sky gradient, a drifting sea band with repeating wave-line motifs, planked boardwalk foreground so no phase plays on dead space; motion/feedback beats: coin-sparkle burst + register pulse on every sale, white froth sweep when the tide returns, shelf items pop-scale when stocked, patron patience meters flash red before walk-out
concept.differentiation_notes: Unlike Moonlighter/Recettear there is no combat half — gathering is itself a timed push-your-luck dash against the tide, and the hook is the cashier counter: triaging a live patience queue with your own handcrafted stock, a loop creature-0001's pure survival foraging never enters.
